messages.content:
Blake Maher used to be a camp counselor.
And one former camper especially stands out in his memory. “Jake” was 8 when he arrived at
Maher’s camp in
California. The first-time camper was shy and uneasy. Soon, though, Jake saw that he had a lot in common with the other campers. And Kids reach new heights at SeriousFun camps. Credit:
he began to join in the
SeriousFun Children’s Network
fun. He even tried the camp’s zipline, Maher remembers. On the last day ot camp, the once-reluctant camper received the “I’ll Try Anything” award.
Jake is just one of thousands of campers with serious illnesses who have experienced a SeriousFun camp. Each camp of its kind is part of the SeriousFun Children’s Network. The group’s mission is to bring joy to kids dealing with medical conditions
On the surface, SeriousFun camps look like typical summer camps.
There are s’mores, campfires, and sing-alongs. Activities like archery, boating, music, and woodshop fill campers’ days. But these camps offer much, much more. At SeriousFun camps, kids can experience independence, adventure, and a sense of belonging. The camps bring to life the vision of the famous actor Paul Newman.
It all started with salad dressing. In 1982, Newman decided to market his Newman’s Own Italian dressing in grocery stores. It was a hit.
Then, Newman decided to give away all the profits. In 1988, these funds helped to open the Hole in the Wall Gang Camp in Connecticut.
Newman’s idea was the start of something big. Today, there are more than 30 Seriousfun camps and partner programs in 19 countries.
SeriousFun camps offer recreational experiences in a safe environment. Often, they let campers do things they’ve never done before. Double H (which stands for “Health” and “Happiness”) Ranch in New York has a high ropes course with a bucket seat. This enables campers who use wheelchairs or walkers to climb to the top. Victory Junction in North Carolina has an accessible water park. At each camp, the main goal is to have fun. But every SeriousFun camp also has a medical center for kids who need treatment away from home.
SeriousFun camps can offer life-changing experiences for kids dealing with illness. Take Sophie, a 13-year-old former camper. About her time at the Painted Turtle in California, she said, “Camp is like family to me. There’s no place else on earth where I feel so included.”
As for Blake Maher? He’s now the CEO of SeriousFun Children’s Network. He’s helping many more kids experience what many former campers call “the magic of camp.”

The SeriousFun Children’s Network is a group of camps for kids with serious illnesses. These camps offer traditional summer activities like s’mores, archery, and boating. However, they also offer much more, like a sense of belonging, adventure, and independence. SeriousFun camps aim to bring joy to kids who are dealing with medical conditions. 

The idea for SeriousFun camps came from actor Paul Newman, who decided to give away all the profits from his Newman’s Own Italian dressing. In 1988, these funds helped to open the first camp in Connecticut. Today, there are over 30 SeriousFun camps and partner programs in 19 countries. 

Each camp also has a medical center for kids who need treatment away from home. But the main goal is always to have fun. For many campers, the experience of being at camp can be life-changing. In the words of one former camper, “Camp is like family to me. There’s no place else on earth where I feel so included.” 

The CEO of SeriousFun Children’s Network, Blake Maher, used to be a camp counselor. He’s now helping many more kids experience the magic of camp.

The article talks mainly about how SeriousFun camps
A. provide campers with activities like water parks and ropes courses
B. offer medical centers for children who need treatment at camp
C. deliver fun camp experiences to children with serious illnesses
D. are funded in part by money made from selling salad dressing
The article mainly talks about how SeriousFun camps deliver fun camp experiences to children with serious illnesses.

Think about the article. In what way are Double H Ranch and Victory Junction different?
2/8
A. Double H Ranch is in Connecticut, but Victory Junction is in New York.
B. Double H Ranch has a water park, but Victory Junction has a ropes, course.
C. Double H Ranch is in North Carolina, but Victory Junction is in New York.
D. Double H Ranch has a ropes course, but Victory Junction has a water park.
D. Double H Ranch has a ropes course, but Victory Junction has a water park.

Based on information in the article, which of these happened first?

A. The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp opened in
Connecticut
B. Paul Newman decided to give away all of his profits.
C. SeriousFun camps and programs spread across the world.
D. Paul Newman began to market his salad dressing.
D. Paul Newman began to market his salad dressing.

Which is the closest antonym for the word typical, as it is used in the article?
4 / 8
A. unique
B. average
C. distant
D. favorite
A. unique

In the article, former camper Sophie said:
"Camp is like family to me. There's no place else on earth where I feel so included.!
The author probably included this quote to help the reader understand
A. that SeriousFun camps have locations around the world
B. how SeriousFun camps give campers unique experiences
C. how SeriousFun camps have a positive effect on campers D. that SeriousFun camps are for children with illnesses
C. how SeriousFun camps have a positive effect on campers

Suppose Daniela wants to find out about campers who have attended SeriousFun camps. Daniela would find most of the information
A. on a website sharing SeriousFun campers' stories
B. in a brochure describing SeriousFun camps* activities
C. in a survey asking about SeriousFun campers' interests
D. on a map showing SeriousFun camps' many locations
A. on a website sharing SeriousFun campers' stories.

Which of these is an opinion?
A. SeriousFun camps have medical centers that can provide on-site care for campers
B. SeriousFun camps have the most exciting activities and experiences of any camp.
C. SeriousFun Children's Network has a mission to bring joy to children with illnesses.
D. SeriousFun Children's Network offers camps and partner programs all over the world.
B. SeriousFun camps have the most exciting activities and experiences of any camp.

In this article, children living with illnesses are given the chance to go to summer camps that include everyone.
Think about some of the activities you like to do. What are some ways those activities could be changed to include everyone? Explain.

As an AI language model, I don't have any personal preferences for activities as a human does, but I can certainly provide some ideas on how activities can be changed to include everyone. For instance, games can be modified to make them more accessible to children who may have mobility-related health issues. This can be done by changing the rules to allow for more inclusive play, and by providing accommodations such as adapted equipment or support from volunteers. Activities such as arts and crafts can also be made more inclusive by offering multiple ways for children to participate, such as by providing writing or typing materials for children who may have difficulty using their hands. Lastly, social events can be structured in a way that encourages inclusive interactions, such as by assigning buddy pairs or promoting group activities.
Write a summary of the article. The summary should include the article's main idea and only the most important information, leaving out less important details.

SeriousFun Children's Network is a group of camps designed for children with serious illnesses. These camps provide traditional activities such as archery, boating, and music, but also aim to bring joy and a sense of belonging to children who typically face medical conditions. The SeriousFun camps were started by Paul Newman, who used profits from his salad dressing to open the first camp in the network. Today, there are over 30 SeriousFun camps and partner programs in 19 countries around the world. The camps have medical centers for children who need treatment, and each participant is given the chance to experience adventure and independence. Campers who have attended these camps say that they are like family, providing a positive impact on their lives.
